title: react-instantsearch import fails in jest jsdom environment since 7.11.0
body:
### 🐛 Current behavior

After upgrading to `react-instantsearch@7.11.0`, our tests started to fail with the following error:

```
SyntaxError: Unexpected token 'export'
```

When investigating the issue, we found out it was introduced with [this change](https://github.com/algolia/instantsearch/compare/react-instantsearch-core@7.10.0...react-instantsearch-core@7.11.0#diff-ab6f88a95338456e6f91ca8aa8699155153f09b67f8b2aab1bca4708e47eb9bf), as now `../widgets` is imported instead of `../widgets/index/index`, and where [./answers/answers](https://github.com/algolia/instantsearch/blob/3707501a19e582e8263a3ae5a9d51ff3f5263361/packages/instantsearch.js/src/widgets/index.ts#L3) is imported, where [preact is imported](https://github.com/algolia/instantsearch/blob/3707501a19e582e8263a3ae5a9d51ff3f5263361/packages/instantsearch.js/src/widgets/answers/answers.tsx#L4).

Preact knows about this issue (https://github.com/preactjs/preact/issues/4406), but for now there is no plan on fixing it.

For now, I managed to get around this issue by adding a [testEnvironmentOptions.customExportConditions](https://jestjs.io/docs/configuration#testenvironmentoptions-object) jest configuration to our repository:

```js
testEnvironmentOptions: {
  customExportConditions: ['require'],
}
```

### 🔍 Steps to reproduce

1. Clone https://github.com/chrisvltn/algolia-jsdom-issue
2. Run `npm install`
3. Run `npm run test`

### Live reproduction

https://github.com/chrisvltn/algolia-jsdom-issue

### 💭 Expected behavior

Tests to pass without runtime error.

### Package version

react-instantsearch@7.11.0

### Operating system

_No response_

### Browser

_No response_
